info.topictitle: Forms of Government in Italian City States

markdown:
****

![Italian City States](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/d5652ef1b23163c8c78b11863ae31f503542eba2-401x401.jpg)

## **Milan**


![ ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/170de8f6de013366a88ff02472b260e378454b12-8192x5107.png)

1. Came under the control of powerful noble families, most notably the **Visconti** (1277–1447) and later the **Sforza dynasty** (from 1450).
2. Turned Milan into a principality where **power was concentrated in one ruler and his family.**
3. Marked by **centralized authority, military strength, and dynastic politics**, rather than republican institutions.
4. The dukes used **mercenary armies (condottieri)** and patronage of artists and scholars to maintain control and project prestige.

## **Florence**


![ ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/5de791d927aac7e7e8041e687a8bd95d6698d381-8192x6686.png)

1. Florence was formally a **republic**, but in practice it was **dominated by powerful families and guilds.**
2. Political participation **limited to wealthy elites.**
3. The **Medici family** manipulated republican institutions and used **patronage networks** to secure loyalty.

## **Venice**


1. Venice developed a **stable oligarchic republic** dominated by the patrician class, whose membership was tightly controlled.
2. The **Doge**, elected for life, was the **ceremonial head of state.**
3. Venice’s government emphasized **collective rule, stability, and continuity**, avoiding the political upheavals common in Florence and Milan.

<callout type="warning">* Treating all** Italian city-states** as the same type of government. 
* Listing rulers (e.g., Visconti, Medici, Doges) without explaining how their leadership affected the **form and stability of government.**
* Answering about one city-state in detail but failing to compare and contrast across all three.</callout>

<callout type="exam_technique">* Organize essays thematically (e.g., “republican institutions,” “role of elites,” “stability and instability”).
* Show how Florence stayed a republic in name but shifted toward Medici oligarchy, while Venice maintained stability, and Milan became a hereditary principality.</callout>

<callout type="self_review">* Compare and contrast the** forms of government **in Milan, Florence, and Venice during the Renaissance.
* To what extent were Italian city-states truly republican in the 15th century
* Assess the role of **powerful families and elites **in shaping the governments of Renaissance Italy.</callout>
